programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The state of Karen Read's intimate relationship with the victim, John O'Keefe, was crucial to the prosecution's case. She was accused of killing him in a hit-and-run after a night of drinking. On the stand, Brian Higgins claimed the defendant planted a kiss on me as he was leaving her home after a visited. Jurors learned about how flirtatious things got between Higgins and read just weeks before O'Keefe was killed.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Text messages dominated this portion of the trial. Karen Read had been sending flirtatious messages to the witness on the stand, Brian Higgins. This was in the weeks before her boyfriend, John O'Keefe, was killed. Next, the cross-examination began on Higgins. The Defense wanted to discredit his testimony in an attempt to show jurors that he had more to hide in his role in O'Keefe's death.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense grilled the man who she was flirting with in text messages. The jury was shown these texts between Karen Read and Brian Higgins weeks before John O'Keefe was killed. During cross examination, the Defense was trying to prove that Higgins was romantically interested in read and got in the middle of her relationship with her boyfriend. Higgins denied that claim but admitted he was attracted to the defendant.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury listened to what Brian Higgins was doing in the hours before John O'Keefe died.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Everybody was in shock the day John O'Keefe died according to Brian Higgins, who was under cross examination. He said he remembered how upsetting that day was, which made his memory a bit fuzzy. Higgins recalled that Karen Read and her boyfriend never showed up to the house party after they told friends gathered at a local bar that they would. Read's defense was convinced that John O'Keefe was beaten up by men at the Albert family home and then dumped in the snow.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Getting Rid Of Cellphone And SIM Card
programme.desc: Karen Read's attorney accused a State witness of changing his statements to law enforcement.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Brian Higgins Wraps Up His Testimony
programme.desc: An ATF special agent admitted that he got rid of cell phone records of the texts he made in the weeks and even hours leading up to the death of John O'Keefe. The long testimony of Brian Higgins wrapped up. He told the jury about his friendship with Karen Read and John O'Keefe, his access to the Canton Police Department, the flirty texts he exchanged with the defendant and how he got rid of the SIM card from his phone.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The doctor who pronounced John O'Keefe dead at the hospital testified. Emergency room physician, Dr. Justin Rice, told jurors how John O'Keefe lost his life in a blizzard. He claimed that O'Keefe did not have a pulse when he got to the ER and was completely unresponsive. When O'Keefe was rushed to a nearby hospital he was declared deceased. His cause of death was blunt force trauma and hypothermia.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Pathologist from the hospital where John O'Keefe was pronounced dead took the stand.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The State was not finished breaking down the testing done on Karen Read when it came to how much alcohol was in her system. Police wanted to know if Karen Read was under the influence of alcohol when she was driving her SUV the night they say she hit John O'Keefe, her boyfriend. He ended up unconscious in the snow for hours. So, investigators used a type of testing to get an estimate of how much alcohol may have been in her system hours before an official blood test was done.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: A city worker who was in charge of scheduling snow plowing in Canton, Massachusetts took the stand.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors listened to more detailed testimony from a lieutenant in the Massachusetts State Police who was responsible for a unit that does evidence searches. His team was assigned to examine the snowbank area where John O'Keefe was found. The Defense conceded that the taillight on Karen Read's SUV was broken at some point. But her lawyers argued that it did not break because she hit O'Keefe. Instead, police planted red and clear plastic at the scene outside the Albert home to frame Read.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors saw crime scene photos during the State's case against her. Massachusetts State Police claimed when they searched the snowy area where John O'Keefe was found, they came across damaged pieces of a car taillight and John's Nike sneaker. Later, the cross-examination began on Lieutenant Kevin O'Hara. He was assigned to scour the crime scene for evidence in the area where John O'Keefe was found all during blizzard conditions.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense had more questions for the state police Lieutenant on the stand. The goal was to poke holes in the investigation into the death of John O'Keefe. According to Read's attorneys, investigators intentionally mishandled this case to frame her. Lieutenant Kevin O'Hara testified that he was unaware the crime scene was left unattended for hours before he began his search.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a series of bank robberies in Texas baffled authorities. But when similar thefts occurred the next year in California, FBI agents linked the crimes and established a modus operandi.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cross-country crime spree over a Labor Day weekend terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and teenage boy disappeared within hours of one another. The police suspected the cases were linked.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country in a wave of abductions and car thefts. When a foreign military officer disappeared along the trail, the FBI believed the pair were growing more violent.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Los Macheteros terrorist organization sought to end U.S. rule of Puerto Rico through violence. When the group claimed responsibility for an attack on 18 unarmed servicemen traveling near San Juan, the FBI is called in to investigate the killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the upscale town of Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl was taken from her home. When the kidnapper's car was discovered in a field near the town, the FBI and local police teamed up with hundreds of volunteers in their search for the girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Amid the cocaine wars of 1980s Miami, a multi-agency tactical squad suspected that a group of cops might be involved in illegal drug busts. The squad uncovered a ring of police who were making millions by ripping off smuggled cocaine.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1996, two men entered a pawnshop in Birmingham, Alabama and callously murdered the employees.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In January 1984, a U.S. Customs officer disappeared from his post near the Del Rio, Texas border crossing, prompting local police to search for four Latin American suspects. The following day, the official was found dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a parole officer was found shot dead in his car, the FBI, DEA and NYPD struggled to contain the mounting violence from drug gangs that continued to escalate. Authorities sought to put two ruthless drug kingpins behind bars for the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Angered by the government's policy in the Middle East, Mir Qazi brutally gunned down two members of the CIA as they waited in their cars at a stoplight. The killer fled the country and was placed on the FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charles Albright's entire life revolved around crime. He graduated from being a small-time crook, into a child-molester and then evolved into a serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Teen Mhairi was killed on her walk home after a festive night. Investigators traced her last steps, using CCTV and forensics that pointed to Gavin, a violent repeat offender whose history revealed how dangerous he truly was.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Lonnie David Franklin Jr. is better known by the nickname 'Grim Sleeper.' He lured vulnerable women into his car before murdering and sexually assaulting them for more than two decades.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, mutilated and killed at least eight women in the US.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2022, Danville Neil was finally jailed for the brutal 1993 murders of a WWII veteran and his sister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Goudeau terrorized victims in the Phoenix metro area between August 2005 and June 2006. In addition to committing nine murders, his extensive crime spree included 84 other felony crimes, totaling 93 felonies over the ten-month period.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Joshua Wade had all the hall marks of a killer. We'll never understand the motives behind his random killings.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dublin man Eric Locke refused to accept it when Sonia Blount, a mother of one, ended their relationship. After resorting to begging, stalking, threats and manipulation, he created a fake social media profile, posing as a potential love interest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A man is jailed for the murder of an author, which leads to a second conviction for the death of his wife.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Police in Aurora, Colorado found the body of a young man in in his own home with a bullet wound to the head.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In April 1997, 27-year-old Rachel McGrath was stabbed to death by an unknown attacker in a car park. 24 hours later and 100 miles away, a terrified 17-year-old student flagged down a motorist, telling them she'd just escaped from a man.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: "Pee Wee" grew from a petty criminal into a violent predator. When a teen disappeared, detectives uncovered his escalating brutality, realizing he had far more blood on his hands than anyone suspected.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: New Jersey native Richard Kuklinski ran a burglary ring, distributed pirated pornography and dealt in weapons and drugs. He also claimed to have murdered over 100 people while working as a hit man for New York and New Jersey's crime families.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Gary Vinter, in prison for double murder, stabs a fellow inmate in the eye and gives another brain damage.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After murdering his aunt and uncle, Ramon Escobar rampages across L.A. and strangles his cellmate in prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Willing to kill for just $20 and drugs, Joshua Gardner commits his first murder at 24. Even behind bars, he can't contain his rage, brutally beating a fellow inmate to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Denver Simmons is as dangerous as he is ruthless. Incarcerated for murdering a woman and her 14-year-old son, his violence only spiraled further once he was behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: From 1985 to 1988, 18 people were choked, molested and left for dead in the remote mountains of California.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On September 17, 1984, in a suburb of Tucson, Arizona, eight-year-old Vicki Hoskins left home on her pink bicycle to mail a letter for her mother. She never returned, but her slightly damaged bicycle was found nearby.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are dispatched to the scene of a shooting, and discover the victim's husband is one of their own: a homicide detective who says his wife accidentally shot herself in the head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While at a real estate conference in St. Louis, a man finds his wife drowned in the bathtub in their hotel room.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An 11-year-old Boy Scout comes down with a fever and diarrhea after eating an undercooked burger. When his condition worsens, his mother drives him to a hospital and doctors discover E. coli destroying his body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After 6-year-old Michelle Lee Dorr went missing, her father confessed to murdering her. But investigators soon poked holes in his story, leaving the case unsolved for 14 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder investigation in Florida crosses jurisdictions from New York and Jamaica. The police rely on cell phone mapping, wiretapping and a host of forensic evidence to link a suspect to a murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A neighbor watched as a man stabbed his wife, pushed her into the swimming pool and held her head under water.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a surgeon is found dead in his home, police assume it was suicide. The investigation reveals otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A drive-by shooting leaves one man dead and another seriously wounded. Cell phone calls and shell casings point to a suspect, but authorities are unable to place him at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months passed, then years, and the crime was never solved.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered among garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight takes the life of a national wrestling champion, police must determine if he was killed in cold blood or in self-defense.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 17-month-old Josh Hinson died in a fire which a federal agency ruled was intentionally set by Josh's mother Terri Strickland. Using the then-emerging resources of the Internet, Terri Strickland undertook her own investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police charge a mentally unstable man with his wife's murder, but a medical examiner discovers that she died of natural causes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, Marie Robards suffered the loss of her father due to a heart attack - but it was more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lisa Manderach and her daughter Devon left their Pennsylvania home to go shopping but didn't return home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother claims that an intruder murdered her two sons, but lacking a motive, police use blood-spatter analysis and behavioral profiling to reconstruct a different scenario.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, when off-duty Maine State Trooper Vicky Gardner is attacked by Steven Fortin during a routine stop, it triggers a chain of events which jumpstarts a stalled murder case in New Jersey.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: British detectives worked with a pioneering scientist to solve crimes of sexual assault and serial murder. This 1986 case marked the first time DNA was used as evidence in a court of law.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is killed while walking her dogs in the woods. Since she has no enemies and her husband has an alibi, police assume it's a hunting accident - until they find a strange letter, allegedly written by the woman before her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Robert Sims returned home after working the night shift and found his wife, Paula, unconscious on the kitchen floor. Their two-year-old son, Randy, was asleep in an upstairs bedroom, but their six-week-old daughter, Heather, was missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Shortly after daybreak in Vancouver, British Columbia, a fire was set in a dumpster. No one saw either the arsonist or the fire, and it burned for hours in the deserted parking lot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car is found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the night of May 22, 1992, Betty Wilson returned home and walked up the stairs to the bedroom. She discovered her husband lying in a pool of blood. Jack Wilson had obviously been murdered...but how? And by whom? Even the experts couldn't agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1987 disappearance of Houston resident Tracy Jo Shine is recalled. The investigation went cold until 2000 when a "cold case squad" learned that the woman's ex-boyfriend Michael Neal had bragged about killing her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman's bone marrow test reveals arsenic poisoning, investigators try to determine if it is due to groundwater contamination or something more sinister.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman with felony warrants is staying at a known drug house. The occupants of a vehicle in a routine traffic stop claim to be sovereign citizens and that the law doesn't apply to them. A burglary at a business complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police search for a robbery suspect who has a felony warrant for his arrest. Deputies respond to a call from a mother who claims her son is attacking her. A traffic stop leads to a drug possession charge.

programme.sub-title: I Broke Into the Vet's Office to Get a Pack of Cigarettes

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputy responds to a report of shoplifting. The search of a motel room yields incriminating evidence.

programme.desc: A bear is reported to be wandering near people's homes. Deputies respond to a domestic violence call.

programme.desc: Police respond to a report of a stabbing at a housing complex.
